explicit_text: My dad often talks about Mexico, especially how it was before the cartels arrived in his area. He remembers playing outside until late and excelling in sports during middle school. Despite the hardships, like helping support his large family, Mexico remains a place full of warm memories for us. Even though he hasn't been back in over 20 years, the huaraches he wears remind him of his beloved homeland.
implicit_text: My dad often talks about the country where he grew up, especially how it was before the cartels arrived in his area. He remembers playing outside until late and excelling in sports during middle school. Despite the hardships, like helping support his large family, that place remains full of warm memories for us. Even though he hasn't been back in over 20 years, the huaraches he wears remind him of his beloved homeland.

explicit_text: The Mexican drug war deeply affected my family’s life in Mexico. When cartels started creeping into our area, they even came to buy our land, and eventually sent death threats to my dad and uncles. This violence forced them to flee, carrying only essentials as they escaped the gang violence and poverty. It was a dangerous and unpredictable time that pushed my family to seek safety and a new life in the United States.
implicit_text: The violent conflict involving drug cartels deeply affected my family’s life in their home country. When these criminal groups started creeping into our area, they even came to buy our land, and eventually sent death threats to my dad and uncles. This violence forced them to flee, carrying only essentials as they escaped the gang violence and poverty. It was a dangerous and unpredictable time that pushed my family to seek safety and a new life in the United States.

explicit_text: My dad crossed the Mexican-American border multiple times in the early 2000s, each journey filled with hope and risk. The first time, he and his brothers came to the U.S. to find work and build a new life, settling in North Carolina. He was even detained once but was miraculously released, and he kept crossing to help his siblings and others from his hometown. The border was both a barrier and a gateway to opportunity for our family.
implicit_text: My dad crossed the border between his home country and the U.S. multiple times in the early 2000s, each journey filled with hope and risk. The first time, he and his brothers came to the U.S. to find work and build a new life, settling in North Carolina. He was even detained once but was miraculously released, and he kept crossing to help his siblings and others from his hometown. That border was both a barrier and a gateway to opportunity for our family.

explicit_text: North Carolina became home to my dad and family after they crossed the border. He worked on horse ranches in central North Carolina and later started his own landscaping and irrigation business there. It’s where he met my mom and began raising our family. The skills he gained working on ranches helped him succeed as a business owner, and North Carolina is where our new life took root.
implicit_text: This southeastern U.S. state became home to my dad and family after they crossed the border. He worked on horse ranches in the central part of the state and later started his own landscaping and irrigation business there. It’s where he met my mom and began raising our family. The skills he gained working on ranches helped him succeed as a business owner, and this state is where our new life took root.
